D7 Print L5 Evaluate Reference

G7 Calibration: Neutral Print Density

G7 calibrates a press to a defined shape of neutral grey from paper white to maximum density — not just the midtone, the whole curve

IDEAlliance's G7 method does not target individual CMYK dot gains; it targets the *shape* of the press's neutral grey output. A printed CMY ramp must follow the NPDC (Neutral Print Density Curve) and stay near a* = b* = 0 from highlight to shadow. If the press drifts, the calibrator builds CMY balance curves that snap the measured grey ramp back onto the spec.

Push the press off-neutral and the grey ramp drifts into a cast and bends off the NPDC. Switch G7 calibration on and the snap-back curves pull the measured ramp onto the spec — the cast clears and the density curve straightens.
